hypothesis: If a reinforcement-learning generative model maximizes CATS-based pharmacophoric similarity to known estrogen receptor antagonists while penalizing MACCS/MAP4-based structural similarity (alongside QED), then it will produce molecules with superior ER docking scores and higher novelty without loss of drug-likeness compared to a QED+QVina baseline because preserving key pharmacophore patterns maintains binding interactions while penalizing structural similarity drives exploration of new chemotypes.
